name: trevorc2
summary: TrevorC2 - Command and Control via Legitimate Behavior over HTTP

documentation.readme: TrevorC2 - Command and Control via Legitimate Behavior over HTTP
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/trevorc2 && python3 trevorc2.py --help`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/trevorc2 && cat README.md`; `cd toolboxes/trevorc2 && ls -la`. TrevorC2 - Command and Control via Legitimate Behavior over HTTP.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'post_exploit' phase against surfaces [web, shell_windows, shell_linux] in the Command and Control category. Fits when the engagement needs: TrevorC2 - Command and Control via Legitimate Behavior over HTTP.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: TrevorC2 - Command and Control via Legitimate Behavior over HTTP.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Index entry in the Command and Control category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.
Pairs with the Command and Control system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.
Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.
Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.
Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.
